title: Microscopic Magnetism of A(TiO)Cu4(PO4)4 (A = Ba, Pb, Sr): 31P and 63,65Cu NMR Study
authors: Riho Rästa, Ivo Heinmaa, Joosep Link, Yusuke Kousaka, Tsuyoshi Kimura, Yoshihiko Ihara, Kenta Kimura, Raivo Stern
abstract: We report a comprehensive NMR study of the chiral square-cupola antiferromagnet Pb(TiO)Cu$_4$(PO$_4$)$_4$ and compare its microscopic hyperfine and local-field parameters with the Ba/Sr analogues in the $A$(TiO)Cu$_4$(PO$_4$)$_4$ family. Above $T_{\rm N}\simeq 6.7$ K, the $^{31}$P Knight shift tracks the bulk susceptibility and yields nearly isotropic transferred hyperfine couplings $H_{\rm hf}^{[010]}=6.77(3)$ and $H_{\rm hf}^{[001]}=6.19(3)$ kOe/$μ_{\rm B}$. Below $T_{\rm N}$, the frequency-swept $^{31}$P spectrum splits into three lines, in contrast to the four-line pattern reported for BaTCPO. The line separation tracks the onset of the static $^{31}$P internal field with a power-law exponent $β\simeq 0.23$, consistent with quasi-two-dimensional criticality. Crystal-rotation $^{31}$P NMR in the ordered state resolves all eight symmetry-related P sites and their site-dependent anisotropy. In the ordered state, zero-field $^{63,65}$Cu NMR gives a Cu-site internal field $B_{\rm int}=14.50(6)$ T and a quadrupole frequency $ν_Q=32.72(5)$ MHz, while point-charge electric-field-gradient calculations including Sternheimer corrections yield an on-site Cu hole occupancy $n_d=0.20(4)$, consistent with a ligand-hole-dominated charge-transfer character. Comparing PbTCPO with BaTCPO and SrTCPO, we find that the transferred hyperfine coupling $H_{\rm hf}$ varies across the series, reflecting changes in local Cu-O-P covalency, whereas the ordered-state $^{31}$P internal field in PbTCPO is $69.5$ mT, considerably higher than in BaTCPO ($35.6$ mT) and SrTCPO ($34.6$ mT). This enhancement is not captured by dipolar terms alone and points to the combined effects of transferred contributions and stacking-dependent cancellation.
summary: This paper uses NMR spectroscopy to investigate the microscopic magnetic properties of a family of square-cupola cuprates. It demonstrates how changing the A-site cation (Ba, Pb, Sr) alters the magnetic stacking and local covalency, providing insight into the electronic structure of these charge-transfer insulators.
main_problem: The study aims to characterize the microscopic magnetism and electronic structure of the chiral square-cupola antiferromagnet Pb(TiO)Cu4(PO4)4 and compare it with its Ba and Sr analogues.
main_result: The researchers found that PbTCPO exhibits enhanced 31P internal fields due to ferroic stacking that prevents interlayer field cancellation, and confirmed the materials are ligand-hole-dominated charge-transfer insulators.
method: Single-crystal 31P NMR, zero-field 63,65Cu NMR, and crystal-rotation NMR were used alongside point-charge electric-field-gradient calculations.
model_or_system: The physical system is the A(TiO)Cu4(PO4)4 family of tetragonal square-cupola cuprates, specifically focusing on the Pb, Ba, and Sr variants.
key_observables: 31P Knight shift, 31P internal field, 63,65Cu internal field, quadrupole frequency, and hyperfine coupling tensors.
important_parameters: Neel temperature (TN approx 6.7 K), exchange scale (J/kB approx 8.0 K), and Cu hole occupancy (nd approx 0.20).
main_assumptions: Point-charge EFG calculations include Sternheimer corrections to account for the electronic environment.
figures_summary: The notes do not provide a specific description of the figures, but they imply data regarding Knight shift anisotropy, line splitting, and EFG-related parameters.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the square-cupola family, details the NMR and magnetometry experimental setup, presents the 31P NMR results regarding hyperfine coupling and criticality, discusses 63,65Cu ZFNMR results for local fields and EFG, and concludes with a comparison of the A-site substitution effects.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1: Temperature dependence of magnetic suscepti- bility χ(T) of PbTCPO (black B∥[010] and red B∥[001]), BaTCPO (navy B∥[100] and magenta B∥[001]) single crystals, and SrTCPO (violet) powder sample in an ap- plied magnetic field Bext = 4.7 T. The cyan and green lines represent the Curie–Weiss fits for 50 - 300 K. The inset shows splitting at TN = 6.7 K for PbTCPO.

FIG. 3: Magnetic field dependence of the phase tran- sition temperature in PbTCPO with the single crystal c axis ([001]) aligned with B. The black circles show the phase transition TN from room temperature to the ordered state; the black dots and pink dots show a two- step transition from the low-field (LF) ordered state to the field-induced (FI) ordered state. The red dots are from Ref. 18 for comparison.
FIG. 2: PbTCPO temperature dependence of magnetic susceptibility χ(T) at the field-induced phase transition region at varying magnetic fields, measured in the direc- tion B∥[001].
FIG. 4: 31P Knight shift temperature dependence of PbTCPO single crystal for (a) B∥[010] and (b) B∥[001]. Insets: Clogston–Jaccarino plots K vs χ with linear fits; the slope gives Hhf.
FIG. 5: Temperature dependence of the 31P line splitting ∆F for (a) B ∥[010] and (b) B ∥[001] below TN. Insets: fits of ∆F(T) using Eq. (3) plotted versus x = 1 − T TN .
